title: Atractocarpus ngoyensis

The plant's classification is subject to ongoing research and potential revision.

intro: Atractocarpus ngoyensis, also known as Ngoye tree, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, native to tropical forests in East Africa. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Atractocarpus ngoyensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the Ngoye tree is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions.
